analysis.mistake: Mobile menu was not displaying properly after revision 16 structural restructure that moved nav inside the right column for desktop logo spanning.
analysis.expected: Mobile menu should be fully functional and properly positioned across all breakpoints.
analysis.rootCause: The right column was constrained inline on mobile instead of wrapping to full width. Moving nav into the right column worked for desktop (logo spanning two rows) but the flex parent didn't wrap on smaller breakpoints, causing the right column (and thus the mobile menu inside it) to be squeezed inline.
analysis.detailedContext: Revision 16 restructured the header to place nav inside the right column to allow the logo to span the full height on desktop. This fixed the desktop layout but inadvertently broke mobile because the flex parent's children (left column and right column) didn't wrap to stack vertically on mobile. The fix added max-md:flex-wrap to the parent and max-md:w-full max-md:order-last to the right column, causing it to wrap to a second full-width row on mobile while preserving desktop layout.

suggestions.content: **Responsive flex-wrap for structural reflows**: When restructuring flex layouts for desktop optimization (e.g., moving nav into columns for logo spanning), always test mobile wrapping behavior. Use `max-md:flex-wrap` on the flex parent and `max-md:w-full max-md:order-last` on child columns that should reflow to full-width rows on mobile. Desktop `gap` and `flex` classes remain inert on mobile when `max-md:` prefixes are used.
suggestions.rationale: Desktop-optimized structural changes can break responsive layouts if flex wrapping isn't explicitly controlled across breakpoints. This pattern ensures mobile menu accessibility is preserved when restructuring for desktop logo spanning or other desktop layout requirements.
